title: Alignment
pickOne: **Lawful** - Help people resolve a dispute peacefully.
**Good** - Place yourself in danger to defend someone helpless.
**Neutral** - Defeat an opponent stronger than you.
**Evil** - Dominate an enemy, and make sure everyone knows about it.

title: Race
pickOne: **Human** - Most steadings will have temples that are friendly to wandering monks. You may petition them for shelter, and they will almost always give you a place to stay. It won't be luxurious, but you'll have a roof over your head and rice to eat.
**Dwarf** - Your short stature helps you sneak under your enemies' weapons. When you try to get inside of an opponent's reach, take +1.

title: Meditation (WIS)
description: When you **spend a few hours in quiet meditation**, lose all of your current ki and hold 3 ki. You may only use one ki per roll. You cannot use ki if you are wearing any armor.

bullets: Deal an additional +1d6 damage with your next attack
Take +1 forward to your next Spout Lore or Discern Realities move
Roll +WIS instead of +Bond when you Aid Another

title: Monk's Path
description: You follow a strict way of discipline to focus your ki and follow the way to enlightenment. Choose a path from the list. Each path gives you an additional move, a way to mark XP at the end of the session, and a restriction. If you ever violate your path's restriction, you lose 1 ki and take -1 forward.

title: Kensei Weapons
description: Many of the possessions of ancient masters of martial arts contain lingering traces of their powerful ki, long after the master has passed on. Weapons in particular are treasured heirlooms of monasteries. In the hands of a monk, a kensei weapon gains the benefit of any known moves that modify the monk's unarmed strikes.

title: Iron Palm Training
description: Your unarmed strikes have a range of hand and close. You can always safely use unarmed strikes against creatures with dangerous forms. For example, you can hit a creature covered in spikes without taking damage, your hand won't be damaged from punching an iron golem, and striking a fire elemental will not burn you. You still may have to Defy Danger to make an unarmed strike against huge creatures, however.

title: Tiger Claw
description: Your unarmed strikes deal +1d4 damage.

title: Serpent Sting
description: Your unarmed strikes gain the **precise** tag and +1 piercing.

title: Crane Stance
description: Your fighting stance makes you very difficult to move or knock down. You ignore the **forceful** tag on any attacks made on you. Additionally, when you **Defy Danger** by standing your ground and letting the threat flow past you, take +1.

title: Fortitude of the Mountain
description: You no longer require food or drink. When a move would make you use up a dungeon ration, ignore it. Additionally, you can hold your breath for a number of minutes equal to your Constitution score, and a few hours of meditation is as restful as a full night's sleep.

title: Leopard's Grace (+DEX)
description: When you **move across dangerous or difficult terrain with the grace of a jungle cat**, roll+DEX.

bullets: You leave no trace of your passage, and nobody notices you.
You are not harmed or hindered while you move.
You move with near superhuman speed and grace.

title: Still Mind
description: When you attempt to resist a magical effect influencing you, take +1.

title: Inner Peace
description: When you Meditate, hold 4 ki.

title: Water Body
description: When you are about to be damaged by a non-magical ranged attack, you may spend 1 ki to prevent all of the damage.

title: Wind Walker
description: You can jump much higher and farther than a normal person, and you can climb surfaces swiftly without tools. You never take damage from falling.

title: Shooting Stars
description: Your shuriken gain the **flexible** tag, as well as the benefit of the Tiger Claw and Serpent Sting moves, if you have them. Additionally, you may Blot Out the Sun with your shuriken, as per the Ranger move.

title: Finish Him!
description: When you defeat an opponent, if you currently hold less ki than your WIS, hold +1 ki.

title: Sensei
description: When you **Spout Lore**, you may roll with +WIS instead of +INT.

title: Monkey Moves (Requires level 6+)
description: Your unarmed strikes now have a range of reach in addition to hand and close, and gain the **flexible** tag.

title: Dragon Roar (Requires level 6+)
description: Your unarmed strikes have the **forceful** tag, and when you spend ki to deal additional damage with an unarmed strike, deal an extra 1d6 damage.

title: Steel Guard (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: Water Body OR Still Mind

You gain +1 armor. If you have both required moves, you gain +3 armor instead.

title: Wholeness of Body (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: Fortitude of the Mountain

You are highly resistant to poisons and diseases. Only very potent toxins or magical ailments affect you, and even then the effects are reduced.

title: Cloud Steps (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: Leopard's Grace OR Wind Walker

You can balance effortlessly on the smallest or thinnest perches, and walk across the surface of water or even empty air for a few steps. If you have both required moves, when you use Leopard's Grace or Defy Danger with an amazing feat of acrobatics, take +1.

title: Ki Palm (Requires level 6+)
description: Your unarmed strikes count as magical weapons, and can affect creatures that normally require a magical weapon to harm, such as monsters with the insubstantial quality.

title: Mindfulness (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: Sensei

You no longer spend ki to roll +WIS instead of +Bond when you Aid Another.

title: Dairokkan (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: Still Mind

You're never caught by surprise. When an enemy would get the drop on you, you get to act first instead. Additionally, when you Defy Danger, you can spend 1 ki to add +1 to your roll, retroactively.

title: Insightful Meditation (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: Inner Peace

When you Meditate, you receive a useful insight into a problem of your choice, as if you had cast the Cleric spell Revelation. When you gain an insight, you cannot gain another insight until you have dealt with the current problem at hand.

title: Journey of a Thousand Miles (Requires level 6+)
description: You no longer have to spend ki to use the extra move granted by your Monk's Path.
